title: Type should not be examined on System.Type instances
description: Type should not be examined on "System.Type" instancesAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoCallingGetTypeon aTypevariable will always return theSystem.Typerepresentation, which is equivalent totypeof(System.Type). This also applies to passing aTypeargument toIsInstanceOfTypewhich always returnsfalse.In both cases, the results are entirely predictable and should be avoided.ExceptionsCallingGetTypeonSystem.Typeis considered compliant to get an instance ofSystem.RuntimeType, as demonstrated in the following example:typeof(Type).GetType(); // Can be used by convention to get an instance of 'System.RuntimeType'Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitb
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

logic.detection_conditions: Detect GetType() calls on System.Type variables and IsInstanceOfType() with Type arguments, which produce predictable/useless results

conditions: Variable of type Type has GetType() called on it
IsInstanceOfType() is called with a Type argument instead of an object

never_trigger_on: typeof(Type).GetType() - documented way to get RuntimeType
GetType() on regular objects (not Type variables)
logic.reasoning: Calling GetType() on a Type variable always returns System.RuntimeType, which is equivalent to typeof(System.Type). Passing a Type argument to IsInstanceOfType() always returns false. Both patterns indicate a misunderstanding of the reflection API and produce predictable, usually unintended, results.
